verbGlossary.SCHEDULED AS: {source} is represented as one or more detail activities in {target}
verbGlossary.BROKEN INTO: {source} is decomposed into multiple detail activities to capture its installation sequence
verbGlossary.DETAILED IN: {source} has specific start, finish, and duration defined at {target} level
verbGlossary.ROLLED UP INTO: {source}d under a summary {target} or WBS node for reporting
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source}of {target} creates a milestone event that enables downstream work to begin
verbGlossary.MARKS COMPLETION OF: {source}'s final acceptance is the defining event for a contractual or phase {target}
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED AT: {source} must pass regulatory certification at a defined {target} before occupancy or use
verbGlossary.CODED IN: {source} is assigned a unique activity ID within {target}'s identification system
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIED WITHIN: {source}'s schedule activities carry identification codes that tie back to {target} scope
verbGlossary.PROGRESS TRACKED BY: {source}'s percent complete and remaining duration are monitored through duration and progress tracking
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source}'s installed quantity or completion status is measured against its planned duration
verbGlossary.TAGGED BY: {source}'s activities are assigned {target} codes for filtering, sorting, and reporting
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source} is categorized by {target} codes that identify its trade, area, or system
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED BY: {source}'s position in the construction sequence is defined by relationship types (FS, SS, FF, SF)
verbGlossary.LINKED THROUGH: {source} is connected to predecessor and successor elements vi{target} logic relationships
verbGlossary.PREDECESSOR OF: {source} must be substantially complete before dependent enclosure or finish work can start
verbGlossary.LAGGED BEFORE: A mandatory wait period (lag) is inserted after {target} before the next trade can proceed
verbGlossary.CURE TIME IN: {source} requires a curing or setting period represented as lag time in {target}
verbGlossary.DRY TIME IN: {source} needs a drying interval captured as lag time before subsequent finishes can be applied
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINED BY: {source}'s start or finish is restricted by a mandatory, discretionary, or external dependency
verbGlossary.DEPENDS ON: {source} cannot begin until a prerequisite element or condition is satisfied per {target} logic
verbGlossary.FLAGGED AS OPEN IN: {source} has a missing successor or predecessor, creating an open-end logic gap in {target}
verbGlossary.VALIDATED IN: {source}'s {target} logic is checked against logic density requirements to ensure adequate ties
verbGlossary.WEATHER DEPENDENT IN: {source}'s {target} is governed by a seasonal or weather-sensitive work calendar
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED PER: {source}'s work dates follow the assigned work calendar including shift patterns and holidays
verbGlossary.DATE CONSTRAINED BY: {source} has a hard start or finish date imposed by {target}
verbGlossary.UTILITY CONSTRAINED BY: {source}'s start is held by an external utility connection or approval constraint date
verbGlossary.FORECASTED BY: {source}'s projected start and finish are computed from calculated early and late dates
verbGlossary.STATUSED THROUGH: {source}'s progress has been recorded through the current data date in {target} update
verbGlossary.BASELINED IN: {source}'s planned start and finish are locked in the approved {target} dates for variance tracking
verbGlossary.BUFFERED BY: {source} has {target} or slack that absorbs delays without affecting the project completion date
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source}'s activities sit on {target} and directly control project completion timing
verbGlossary.CRITICAL TO: {source} is on {target} with zero float, making any delay a project-level delay
verbGlossary.NEAR CRITICAL IN: {source}'s {target} is low enough that it appears in near-critical path analysis as a risk
verbGlossary.ANALYZED WITHIN: {source}'s {target} paths are examined through longest path or near-critical analysis methods
verbGlossary.EARNED VALUE IN: {source}'s physical progress is converted to earned {target} metrics for objective measurement
verbGlossary.ORIGINAL DURATION IN: {source}'s first-approved duration is captured in {target} for future comparison
verbGlossary.UPDATED IN: {source}'s actual start, actual finish, or remaining duration is revised in {target} cycle
verbGlossary.REFORECASTED IN: {source}'s projected dates are recalculated in {target} update based on current progress
verbGlossary.PLANNED IN LOOKAHEAD OF: {source}'s near-term installation is detailed in the rolling look-ahead {target}
verbGlossary.STAGED IN: {source}'s material delivery and crew mobilization are coordinated in the look-ahead {target}
verbGlossary.RECOVERED IN: {source}'s delayed activities are re-sequenced or compressed in a recovery {target} to regain time
verbGlossary.CRASH SCHEDULED IN: {source}'s duration is aggressively compressed in the recovery {target} using added resources
verbGlossary.RECORDED IN: {source}'s actual installation dates and durations are documented in the as-built {target} record
verbGlossary.ACTUAL DATES IN: {source}'s real start and finish dates are captured in the as-built {target} for closeout
verbGlossary.CREW LOADED ON: {source}are assigned to {target}'s activities for resource planning
verbGlossary.EQUIPMENT LOADED ON: {source}plant resources are assigned to {target}'s schedule activities
verbGlossary.CASH FLOW TIED TO: {source}'s payment draws and cost forecasts are linked to its {target} dates
verbGlossary.LEVELED ACROSS: {source}'s activities are shifted within float to resolve resource over-allocation across {target}
verbGlossary.SMOOTHED WITHIN: {source}'s resource demand is evened out through {target} timing adjustments during leveling
verbGlossary.WEATHER DELAYED IN: {source}'s work was halted by weather and the delay is categorized as excusable in {target}
verbGlossary.IMPACT ANALYZED ON: {source}quantifies how a change or delay to {target} shifts the project completion
verbGlossary.UTILITY DELAY ON: A delay to {target} caused by external utility availability is analyzed for schedule impact
verbGlossary.FORENSICALLY ANALYZED IN: {source}'s actual vs planned performance is reconstructed in a retrospective delay analysis
verbGlossary.ACCELERATED IN: {source}'s remaining work is compressed through added crews, overtime, or re-sequencing
verbGlossary.OVERTIME APPLIED TO: {source}are applied to {target}'s activities to recover lost schedule time
verbGlossary.ASSESSED IN: {source}'s {target} activities are evaluated as part of the DCMA 14-point quality assessment
verbGlossary.SPEC COMPLIANT IN: {source}'s {target} detail meets the specification requirements for granularity and logic
verbGlossary.CPLI MEASURED ON: {source}is calculated including {target}'s contribution to the critical path
verbGlossary.TIERED ACROSS: {source} appears at multiple {target} levels from summary (L1) down to detail (L4)
verbGlossary.IMPORTED INTO: {source}'s {target} data is brought into the CPM tool (P6, MS Project) from external sources
verbGlossary.LINKED TO DAILY LOG IN: {source}'s {target} activities are connected to daily field reports for plan-vs-actual tracking
verbGlossary.FIELD TRACKED IN: {source}'s installation progress is monitored through {target}-to-field linkage with daily logs
verbGlossary.CHANGE TRACKED ON: Date shifts, duration changes, or logic edits affecting {target} are captured in change tracking
verbGlossary.COMMITTED IN: The installing trade commits to completing {target}'s work in the Last Planner weekly plan
verbGlossary.WEEKLY PLANNED IN: {source}'s installation is planned at a weekly granularity through the Last Planner System
verbGlossary.ZONE PACED IN: {source} is installed at a fixed production rate per zone following takt time planning
verbGlossary.LINEARLY SCHEDULED IN: {source}'s repetitive installation along a route is planned using linear scheduling methods
verbGlossary.STATION SEQUENCED IN: {source} is {target}d by station or chainage in a linear scheduling model
verbGlossary.4D SIMULATED IN: {source}'s installation sequence is animated in a 4D BIM model to visualize constructability
verbGlossary.VISUALLY SEQUENCED IN: {source}'s placement is simulated in 4D/5D BIM to validate {target} logic and spatial conflicts
verbGlossary.PERFORMANCE INDEXED ON: {source}'s {target} progress is measured as a ratio against baseline to produce a performance index
verbGlossary.BEI TRACKED ON: {source}tracks how closely {target}'s actual dates match the baseline plan
verbGlossary.FLOAT ERODING ON: {source}'s available {target} is declining across successive schedule updates, signaling emerging risk
verbGlossary.ADHERENCE RATED ON: {source}'s {target} hit rate is calculated as a percentage of on-time completions
verbGlossary.CRITICAL RATIO ON: {source}'s activities are evaluated against the critical {target} ratio to gauge schedule risk concentration
verbGlossary.PPC SCORED ON: {source}'s weekly {target} completions are measured as Percent Plan Complete in lean tracking
verbGlossary.OUT OF SEQUENCE ON: {source}'s activities are progressing outside the planned logic sequence, creating {target} anomalies
verbGlossary.HEALTH SCORED ON: {source}'s {target} activities contribute to the overall schedule health score assessment
